revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Futahashira Shrine]][[ja:二柱神社]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = Shintoism | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Futahashira jinja worship hall 001.jpg | consecration_year = 1026 |Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Futahashira Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |location = Ichinasaka Nishiumi 61, Izumi-ku, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|38.31901|N|140.88597|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = [[Izanagi]], {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami|1=Izanami}} |Rank = |Foundation = Manju 2 (1026) |Honden = |Other names = Niwatari Daigongen, Obotsuko-sama |Pilgrimage = |Festivals = May 18, November 3 |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Futahashira Shrine|lat_deg=38|lat_min=19|lat_sec=8.4|lon_deg=140|lon_min=53|lon_sec=9.4|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=230|caption=}}Position in Miyagi Prefecture{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=230|coord={{coord|38|19|8.4|N|140|53|9.4|E|}}}}Position in Sendai City |location = {{ill|Izumi-ku|qid=Q1043338|1=Izumi-ku, Sendai}} |deity = {{ill|Izanagi|qid=Q813858|1=Izanagi}}; {{ill|Izanami|qid=Q682306|1=Izanami}} }} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Sendai, Miyagi, Japan}}{{Nihongo|'''Futahashira Shrine'''|二柱神社|Futahashira jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Sendai|en|Sendai|qid=Q46747|lt=Sendai|1=Sendai}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|qid=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}, Japan.<ref>{{Cite web |title=宮城県神社庁 |url=https://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310020345 |access-date=2023-05-04 |website=www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp |language=ja}}</ref> The main [[kami]] enshrined here are [[Izanagi]] and {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami|1=Izanami}}.
==Overview== '''Futahashira Shrine''' (二柱神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Izumi-ku, Sendai|en|Izumi-ku, Sendai|qid=Q1043338|lt=Izumi-ku, Sendai|1=Izumi-ku, Sendai}}, Miyagi Prefecture. The name derives from the enshrinement of two deities.
The shrine is also known as "Niwatari Daigongen" and "Obotsuko-sama" (God of Children), believed to bestow blessings for children’s health and safety<ref name=":0" />. Since the enshrined deities Izanagi and Izanami are a divine couple, the shrine is also revered for blessings related to matchmaking. The name "Daigongen" reflects the influence of the {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|qid=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}} era, while the name "Futahashira Shrine" dates from the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|qid=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} of the Meiji period.
== History == According to the shrine’s records, it was established in Manju 2 (1026) at Shūrindan in Ichinasaka (present-day Shichikashita Higashiura). The original site remains marked as Futahashira Shrine Ruins. During the {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|qid=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} (1573–1591), it became the guardian shrine for eight villages, including Ichinasaka and Matsumori, under the influence of the Kokubun clan. When the Kokubun clan declined and the Date clan ruled, the shrine was relocated in Kanbun 2 (1662) to its present location along the Kankan River following a divination of the sacred ground<ref>{{Cite web|title=Futahashira Shrine Details|url=https://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310020345|publisher=Miyagi Shrine Association|access-date=2022-07-21}}</ref>.
In 1872, it became a village shrine for Shichikashita Village, and its grounds were expanded. In 1929, a fire destroyed the shrine buildings. The current structure was completed in 1941 after over a decade of effort by parishioners amid political turmoil<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Futahashira Shrine History|url=https://www.f-shrine.com/about/index.html|publisher=Official Site|access-date=2022-07-21}}</ref>.
In 2024, the shrine enshrined Hiruko no Mikoto from {{ill|Nishinomiya Shrine|en|Nishinomiya Shrine|qid=Q705297|lt=Nishinomiya Shrine|1=Nishinomiya Shrine}} in Hyōgo Prefecture.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Izanagi]] * {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami|1=Izanami}} * Hiruko
== Grounds == * Haiden and Honden * Kaguraden * Mikoshiden * Auxiliary Shrine: {{ill|Raijin Shrine|lt=Raijin Shrine|lt=Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|雷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikazuchi Shrine|qq=}} :: Dedicated to the thunder deity, this shrine was venerated for rainmaking and agricultural blessings. Relocated to Futahashira Shrine in the 1970s after urban development demolished its original site. * Stone Monuments: Including Bato Kannon and Barekijin <gallery> File:Futahashira jinja 001.jpg|Shrine entrance File:Futahashira jinja 002.jpg|Mikoshiden File:Futahashira jinja Auxiliary Ikazuchi jinja 001.jpg|Auxiliary Shrine: Ikazuchi Shrine File:Futahashira jinja Auxiliary Ikazuchi jinja 002.jpg|Ikazuchi Shrine sacred object File:Futahashira jinja Stone Monument 002.jpg|Stone monuments for Bato Kannon and Barekijin File:Futahashira jinja Stone Monument 001.jpg|Mountain deity, Bato Kannon, and Barekijin monuments </gallery>
== Access == * About a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Yaotome Station|en|Yaotome Station|qid=Q8049075|lt=Yaotome Station|1=Yaotome Station}} or {{ill|Izumi-Chūō Station (Miyagi)|en|Izumi-Chūō Station (Miyagi)|qid=Q6102259|lt=Izumi-Chūō Station|1=Izumi-Chūō Station (Miyagi)}} on the {{ill|Sendai Subway Namboku Line|en|Sendai Subway Namboku Line|qid=Q908156|lt=Sendai Subway Namboku Line|1=Sendai Subway Namboku Line}}. * 3 minutes on foot from Shichikashita Bridge bus stop on {{ill|Sendai Transportation|ja|仙台交通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai Transportation|1=Sendai Kōtsū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11377781}} buses.
== Notes == {{footnotes}} {{reflist}}
{{commonscat|Futahashira jinja}} == External Links == * [https://f-shrine.com/index.html Futahashira Shrine Official Website]
==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan]]
== References == {{Reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.f-shrine.com Official website]
